problem: Form submission analytics must be aggregated for reporting without exposing PII, and immutability must be enforced to prevent analytics tampering
solution: Use append-only storage pattern: insert analytics records, never update. Filter PII fields (email, phone, ssn, password, creditCard) before indexing. Partition analytics by date (YYYY-MM-DD) for efficient time-series queries. Aggregate metrics (count, avg field count, error rate %) per tenantId/formId/dateWindow. Tenant-scope all aggregation queries via tenantId from AsyncLocalStorage.

pattern.step_1: Extract analytics-safe fields from submission (exclude PII)
pattern.step_2: Determine aggregation window (TODAY, WEEK, MONTH) and date partition
pattern.step_3: INSERT analytics record to date-partitioned index (append-only, no updates)
pattern.step_4: Fetch aggregate document for (tenantId, formId, dateWindow)
pattern.step_5: Update or create aggregate: increment submitCount, update avg field count, recalculate error rate %
pattern.step_6: Store aggregate document (upsert: insert if new, update if exists)
pattern.step_7: All queries filtered by tenantId from ALS (DNA-5)

ironRules: IR-1: Append-only storage — insert only, never update analytics records
IR-2: PII exclusion — email, phone, ssn, password, creditCard never indexed
IR-3: Tenant-scoped aggregation via tenantId from ALS (DNA-5)
IR-4: Aggregate metrics: count, avg field count, error rate %
IR-5: Date partitioning for time-series efficiency
